page.content: The BHP Group's December 2023 report evaluates the economics of mining methods, specifically comparing Underground and Open-Pit strategies across various metrics.

lines.text: The BHP Group's December 2023 report evaluates the economics of mining methods, specifically comparing Underground and

page.content: The Underground method is rated as High in operational risk with a recovery rate of 95%, requiring a CAPEX of £500M and an OPEX of £300M, yielding a production volume of 1.2Mtons, a profit margin of 20%, and an IRR of 12.5%. In contrast, the Open-Pit method is rated Medium for operational risk with a recovery rate of 90%, necessitating a CAPEX of £600M and an OPEX of £400M, producing 1.5Mtons, a profit margin of 25%, and an IRR of 15%. The Low-rated Underground method indicates a lower operational risk with a recovery rate of 85%, requiring £400M in CAPEX and £200M for OPEX, producing 1.0Mtons, achieving an 18% profit margin and a 10% IRR. Additionally, a High-rated Open-Pit method presents a very high operational risk with a recovery rate of 92%, a CAPEX of £700M, OPEX of £500M, and a production volume of 1.8Mtons, offering a profit margin of 22% and an IRR of 14%.

page.content: Lastly, the Low-rated Open-Pit method has a moderate operational risk with an 87% recovery rate, requiring £300M in CAPEX, £150M in OPEX, producing 0.8Mtons, a profit margin of 15%, and an IRR of 8%. The critical minerals overview indicates Lithium production of 1,000tons with a usage rate of 67% of demand, Cobalt at 500tons with 50% of demand, and Nickel planned at 800tons with 67% usage, while inactive Rare Earth production is at 200tons, meeting 67% of demand.
